vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant decline. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are in the black pot, but show severe signs of senescence.

plant_audit.P2_P4: Critical failure. The foliage has transitioned from green to brown/necrotic over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P3: One primary leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and potential edge necrosis.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture observed; no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pot, likely a nutrient intervention.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual yellowing of the P3 leaf; P2/P4 stems show progressive browning.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Total collapse of P2/P4 foliage; P3 leaf remains in a state of stasis but appears increasingly fragile.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of high stress. The Pothos (P3) is the only specimen with viable photosynthetic tissue, though it is clearly struggling. The Mexican Mint and Silver Guest (P2/P4) are effectively dead, showing complete loss of turgor and chlorophyll.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in the soil; electronic sensor cabling visible in the frame.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a failing indoor ecosystem. While the Pothos (P3) maintains a singular, albeit chlorotic, leaf, the other specimens (P2/P4) have succumbed to what appears to be a combination of light deprivation and potential root-zone issues. The yellow pot (P1) remains the most stable component of the collection. Immediate intervention is required to prevent total loss of the remaining P3 specimen.
